content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

October 24, 1990

Robert A. Garcia  
637 3rd Ave., Suite B  
Chula Vista, CA 92010

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-603

Dear Mr. Garcia:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning your obligations under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Your letter accurately reflects the advice that I provided to you during our telephone conversation of September 18, 1990. However, as I related to you in our telephone conversation of September 30, 1990, please note, that after further consideration this response changes the advice.

You were an unsuccessful candidate for State Assembly in the June 1990 election. You wish to use campaign funds for an appreciation picnic for your campaign volunteers. Generally, campaign funds may be used for "an election victory celebration or similar event." Section 85802(f)(2). However, at the end of the postelection reporting period following a candidate's defeat, campaign funds become "surplus funds" and may be used only for certain specified purposes. (Section 85807.) An appreciation picnic for campaign volunteers would not fall within one of the specified purposes. (Id.) The postelection reporting period following your defeat ended on June 30, 1990. Therefore in the future you should not use campaign funds for appreciation picnics.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Our File No. A-90-603
Code 2

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Mark T. Morodomi
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MTM:dg
